## [09:10 - 12:16]

access to something that's going to be revolutionary. And that's what's actually going to get them to listen. So if you are considering running webinars for yourself or for your clients, honestly, there's a lot of variables about first of all what you should sell. We have and that's like, that generally comes after we've completely validated the funnel, we validated the offer, the whole mechanism itself is, is completely rock solid. So first, what should you sell if you're considering running a live webinar? So we've done two models. At first, it was all the like mid ticket offers where it's a course, $500 to $2,000. You just like drop the link in the chat at the end of the of the webinar, and then people can go out and check out and buy. And then we also recently started doing the model where we pushed like a high ticket, or a call at the end. And that's generally just like with the Calumny link with a couple qualifiers. ## [12:16 - 15:21]

came from short form content. So think about somebody, if they've watched even 10 short form videos, call it, they've spent maybe 10 minutes consuming your content. Obviously you can get into YouTube and get into long form content, but the majority of his $600,000 a month coaching business was built off of minute long TikToks that he would make in his kitchen. So the reason the webinar has been super effective for him is because he'll get people in and they consume 10 minutes of content and then you have an hour long, hour and a half long training, where now it's like a speed date. You sat down, you had drinks, you had a good dinner and now you're in a very different position. You guys actually know each other and in this case maybe you're ready to, maybe they're ready to buy from you. So it's just like a speed date and then it simplifies the sales process. Everybody likes to talk about shitty closers and shitty setters and this is a way to either cut them out or give them leads that are like absolute laydowns because if you do it where at the end you're directing them to book a call, they've still sat through an hour, hour and a half long training and they know what they're getting themselves into. So they're going to show up to that call, very different from somebody who's like, watch 10 minutes worth of videos. And then lastly, it's efficient if you have a big net of people. Obviously you can sell to a lot of people at one time. So rather than taking 200 sales calls, you can get 2000 people on a webinar and convert more efficiently than you would if you had to take sales calls individually.
